events.title: Diesel surges 20 cents a litre in two days to record high – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Australia eyes new fuel supply from US, Mexico and Asia as diesel prices spike to record high Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Shadow defence minister ‘reluctant’ to listen to Iran over Lebanon’s inclusion in ceasefire deal James Paterson , the shadow minister for defence, said he is “reluctant” to listen to Iran on whether or not Lebanon was included in a ceasefire deal announced yesterday as Israel continues to bombard the country. On the one hand, the United States and Israel say that the ceasefire did not include Lebanon, and on the other hand, the Islamic Republic of Iran is saying that it did include Lebanon. I am personally reluctant to endorse claims made by a country which has sponsored terrorist attacks on Australian soil. Australians are seeing what is happening in a number of countries in the Middle East and responding with compassion. We recognise this is a time when Australians are also doing it tough and we appreciate how they are recognising the depth of the humanitarian crisis and responding. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Diesel surges 20 cents a litre in two days to record high – as it happened

events.title: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
events.description: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month

events.title: Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China
events.description: Beijing’s powerbrokers are credited with winning Iran over, although one analyst says they were ‘pushing an open door’ Middle East crisis – live updates As the world struggles to make sense of what, if anything, was achieved by the ceasefire deal announced by the US and Iran on Tuesday, one major power that stands to win regardless is China. Beijing’s powerbrokers are being credited with pushing Iran towards agreeing to the ceasefire, bolstering its status as a regional mediator. In China’s tightly censored domestic media, articles basking in the glory of China being the grown-up in the room at a time of international crisis were allowed to circulate. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China

events.title: ‘Collective punishment’: At least 182 killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon
events.description: Lebanon’s health ministry says Israeli strikes on Wednesday killed 182 people – the deadliest single day since the start of the Israel–Hezbollah war. Israel launched a wave of strikes on central Beirut and other parts of the country, even as a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran came into effect. Iranian officials say the deal was meant to include Lebanon, but both Israel and Washington insist it does not. FRANCE 24’s Sharon Gaffney speaks to Sami Nader, Director of the Levant Institute for Strategic Affairs, who says Lebanon is facing “collective punishment” from Israel, with the strikes justified by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s claim that Lebanese authorities do not have full control over their territory.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Collective punishment’: At least 182 killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon

events.title: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
events.description: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant

events.title: 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official
events.description: One attacker is dead and two others injured after a gunfight with police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, Turkey's interior minister says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official

events.title: Middle East live: France blasts Israel’s ‘unacceptable’ Lebanon strikes, ceasefire hangs in balance
events.description: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Thursday condemned Israel’s "unacceptable" strikes on Lebanon, which killed 182 people and wounded 890 the day after the US and Iran reached a two-week ceasefire agreement. As Iran and Hezbollah retaliated for the attacks, Barrot warned the Israeli strikes could undermine the fragile ceasefire. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: France blasts Israel’s ‘unacceptable’ Lebanon strikes, ceasefire hangs in balance

events.title: Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal
events.description: Iran says the U.S. must choose between ceasefire or war via Israel as Tuesday's ceasefire deal excludes Tehran-backed terrorist group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal

events.title: Man rescued after two weeks trapped in collapsed Mexico mine
events.description: A man trapped for nearly two weeks in a flooded gold mine in Sinaloa, Mexico, has been rescued by military divers.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Man rescued after two weeks trapped in collapsed Mexico mine

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise
events.description: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. Meanwhile, humanitarian needs are rising, critical infrastructure remains under strain, and the wider economic and global impacts of the crisis continue to mount. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise

events.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
events.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes

events.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
events.description: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms

events.title: Netanyahu fails to address Israel’s failure to achieve war goals in Iran
events.description: Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky says that when Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu finally made a statement on the US-Iran ceasefire, he failed to “address the fact that Israel has not achieved any of its stated war goals, which included regime change in Iran, the destruction of Iran's ability to launch and to produce ballistic missiles” and eliminate the nuclear threat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Netanyahu fails to address Israel’s failure to achieve war goals in Iran

events.title: Iran-US truce on brink as Israel pounds Lebanon, Tehran warns of escalation
events.description: A fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States hung in the balance on Wednesday as Tehran threatened renewed hostilities and Israel launched heavy strikes on Lebanon. Both sides claimed victory, but at least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded, Lebanese authorities said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-US truce on brink as Israel pounds Lebanon, Tehran warns of escalation

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.description: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security

events.title: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
events.description: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by Hurricane Melissa last year.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies

events.title: Oil price rises as markets question durability of Middle East ceasefire
events.description: Gas prices also increase, while global stock markets slip back after strong rally on Wednesday Business live – latest updates Middle East crisis – live updates Oil and gas prices rose on Thursday and Asian stock markets retreated as the two-week ceasefire in Iran looked increasingly shaky , with Israel continuing attacks on Lebanon and the US and Iran threatening a return to military action. A day after the US and Iran announced an 11th-hour ceasefire , including an agreement to reopen the strait of Hormuz, many questions remain and there were signs that the truce was already being broken, causing jitters in the markets. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil price rises as markets question durability of Middle East ceasefire

events.title: Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say
events.description: Authorities in Nigeria's restive north put the death toll at three, but residents and humanitarians point to a higher toll. The attack occurred in an area where kidnapping gangs and Islamist militants are active.
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Nigeria: At least 20 killed in attack, residents say

events.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
events.description: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight

events.title: Energy crisis not over despite relief rally
events.description: Oil prices have plunged and stock markets soared after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Israel and Iran. But regardless of whether the deal holds, the energy crisis unleashed by the war is far from over. Damage to infrastructure in the region means it could take years for oil and gas production to get back to pre-war levels. Meanwhile, global aviation group IATA says supplies of jet fuel will remain tight.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Energy crisis not over despite relief rally

events.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
events.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire

events.title: Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up
events.description: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: how farmers adapt as fertilizer crunch heats up

events.title: 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Ali Vaez, Director of Iran Project & Senior Advisor at the International Crisis Group. While the US sees the outcome as a decisive military victory, Vaez argues that the more consequential story lies in the reconfiguration of power within Iran. From Tehran’s perspective, survival in the face of overwhelming force constitutes a form of victory. At the same time, this war has not left Iran unchanged. Power has tilted toward more hardline actors, particularly the Revolutionary Guard, reshaping the internal hierarchy of the Islamic Republic.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Pakistani military calling the shots: Has connections with IRCG, very good relationship with Trump'

events.title: Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears
events.description: At least 326 humanitarians were killed in the line of duty across 21 countries during 2025, bringing the total killed over three years to over 1,010. The International Red Cross warned the Security Council on Wednesday that “we are losing our humanity in war.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon
events.description: After nearly 40 days of intense hostilities across the Middle East – marked by rising civilian casualties and widespread damage to critical infrastructure – a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been announced. While the UN Secretary-General has welcomed the move as a step toward a broader peace, reports of continued Israeli strikes and mass casualties across Lebanon underscore the fragility of the situation. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon

events.title: Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond?
events.description: Lebanon has declared a national day of mourning after Israeli attacks killed at least 254 people in a single day.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war day 41: What’s happening in Lebanon, Middle East and beyond?

events.title: UK man jailed for sexually abusing nine-year-old step granddaughter has Australian visa reinstated by tribunal
events.description: The man successfully appealed the automatic revocation of his permanent resident visa after being sentenced to 14 months in prison Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An elderly UK man who served a prison sentence for sexually abusing his step granddaughter when she was nine years old has had his Australian visa reinstated by a tribunal because of his “strong ties to Australia”. The man was sentenced to 14 months prison in the Western Australia district court in February 2024 for molesting the girl in the presence of another child. In Australia, the crisis support service Lifeline is 13 11 14. In the US, the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline is 1-800-273-8255. In the UK, Samaritans can be contacted on 116 123. Other international suicide helplines can be found at befrienders.org Continue reading...

events.title: Hundreds search for wolf that escaped from zoo in South Korea
events.description: Local school closes in Daejeon city as hundreds of emergency service and military personnel scour area around O-World theme park where the wolf escaped from Authorities are hunting for a wolf after it escaped from a zoo in Daejeon, a South Korean city with a population of 1.5million. More than 300 people – including firefighters, police and military personnel – are taking part in the search operation, an official from the Daejeon fire headquarters said. Continue reading...

events.title: US-Iran ceasefire: Why Tehran wants to charge ships for crossing the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: As part of its 10-point proposal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran is pushing for ongoing control over the vital Strait of Hormuz – including charging what officials suggest could be as much as $2 million per ship to help the country rebuild its bombed-out infrastructure and maintain control over one of the world's most strategic waterways.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran ceasefire: Why Tehran wants to charge ships for crossing the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
events.description: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. Continue reading...

events.title: Can the ceasefire hold? Fragile US–Iran truce tested ahead of talks
events.description: No apocalypse. Instead, an eleventh-hour ceasefire between Iran and the United States – one that is still being tested – with both sides claiming victory after five and a half weeks of war that may reshape how the world views them.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Can the ceasefire hold? Fragile US–Iran truce tested ahead of talks

events.title: World News in Brief: Death on the Mediterranean, displacement in Afghanistan, ‘One Health’ summit
events.description: Over 180 people are feared dead or missing in the latest shipwrecks on the Mediterranean, according to the UN migration agency, IOM.

events.title: Middle East crisis exposes global energy fault line as UN urges shift to renewables
events.description: The ongoing crisis in the Middle East is exposing a central vulnerability in the global economy: the dependence on fossil fuels flowing through regions affected by conflict, a situation which is strengthening the UN’s case for a faster transition to cheaper, more resilient renewable power.

events.title: As World Cup kick-off nears, a reminder of the power of sport to build bridges and break barriers
events.description: The 2026 World Cup final will take place at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, a few miles away from UN Headquarters where, on Wednesday, elite athletes and sports administrators spoke about the power of football and other international sports to change the world for the better.

events.title: Guinea-Bissau on edge after activist's killing
events.description: The death of activist Vigario Luis Balanta, found with signs of extreme violence, has triggered protests and accusations against Guinea-Bissau's military rulers, raising concerns over repression and media freedom.

events.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost

events.title: Australian spy plane operators in Middle East not sharing intel with US for offensive operations, defence boss says
events.description: Information on drones and other threats being shared but defence chief confirms crew taking ‘active steps’ to only contribute to defensive actions Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian personnel operating a state-of-the-art surveillance plane are filtering information gleaned from the Middle East war to ensure intelligence is not shared with the United States for offensive purposes, the defence force chief says. As the federal government extended the deployment of the E-7 Wedgetail aircraft on Thursday, the chief of Defence, Admiral David Johnston, said the crew were taking active steps to only contribute to defensive operations. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australian spy plane operators in Middle East not sharing intel with US for offensive operations, defence boss says

events.title: France plans 36 billion euro boost to rearmament, nuclear deterrent expansion
events.description: France plans to ⁠add a further 36 ​billion euros to its defence spending between now and 2030 under an updated military planning law that expands its nuclear ​arsenal and boosts missile and drone stocks. The increase reflects mounting security pressures from wars in Ukraine and the Middle East and growing uncertainty over U.S. commitments to NATO under President ​Donald Trump. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France plans 36 billion euro boost to rearmament, nuclear deterrent expansion

events.title: Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war
events.description: In his first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV called for dialogue over domination, quoting Pope Francis' warning on global indifference.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo XIV invokes Pope Francis' final words in Easter plea against growing 'indifference' to war

events.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
events.description: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National News Desk and the Independent ran stories on the US representative. The reports centred on a post by @RepOfSomaliland in reaction to claims by JD Vance that Omar had committed immigration fraud, which echoed prior allegations against the Somali-born Minnesota Democrat that she has vehemently denied. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security Council resolution put forward by Gulf States to ensure safety and security of ships in the Strait of Hormuz - find full coverage here. Meanwhile, displacement remains high, aid access is constrained. Human rights chief Volker Türk says President Trump’s threat to annihilate Iranian civilisation is “sickening”. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Spring break beach trip turns deadly as fiery crash kills 3 teens, leaves 1 fighting for life
events.description: Three Georgia teens killed and one critically injured after a spring break crash on a Florida highway sent their Jeep into a tree line, officials say.

events.title: Hit New Zealand comedy duo Flight of the Conchords reunion gigs sell out in minutes
events.description: Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement announce four shows at Wellington venue Meow Nui from next week – their first gigs since 2018 New Zealand’s self-described “fourth most popular guitar-based digi-bongo-a cappella-rap-funk-comedy-folk duo” Flight of the Conchords sold out their first shows in eight years in minutes this week, sparking a frenzy among fans. Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement formed the musical comedy act in 1998, soaring to worldwide fame off the back of their HBO comedy series of the same name with tunes including Business Time and Hiphopopotamus vs Rhymenoceros. Continue reading...

events.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
events.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

events.title: Inside the secretive country run by a military leader with a cult following
events.description: Few heads of state command a cult following like the young world leader and military ruler of Burkina Faso, President Ibrahim Traoré.

events.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty

events.title: India's digital census prompts fear of hidden agendas

events.title: Pakistan plays major role trying to mediate an end to Iran war

Islamabad has ties to Tehran and Washington and is set to hold talks this weekend.

events.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over Matthew Perry overdose death

events.title: Trump administration's claims of victory in Iran 'really quite hollow', analyst says

The US can stop its attacks for a while but its claims of victory are really quite hollow".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump administration's claims of victory in Iran 'really quite hollow', analyst says

events.title: China says Afghanistan and Pakistan agreed to explore a 'comprehensive solution'

events.title: Missing teen case flips as ‘kidnapped’ girl found alive, built secret life as mom of 3, investigator: report
events.description: An Arizona girl missing since 1994 has been found alive as a married mother of three working for a private investigations firm in Springfield, Missouri, according to a report.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Missing teen case flips as ‘kidnapped’ girl found alive, built secret life as mom of 3, investigator: report

events.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
events.description: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador last year. Since his return, he has been fighting a second deportation to a series of African countries proposed by homeland security officials. Continue reading...

events.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
events.description: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement

events.title: Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas
events.description: Politicians warn party’s pledge to ‘punish’ countries seeking justice for slavery will harm and isolate Britain Commonwealth politicians say they will not back down from seeking reparations as UK public figures, including a former Reform insider, warn the rightwing party’s pledge to “punish” countries seeking justice for slavery would harm and isolate Britain. This week, Reform UK said they would halt visas for nationals of countries formally demanding reparations from Britain if they took power. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Commonwealth leaders vow to keep seeking reparations after Reform UK plan to halt visas

events.title: Iran doubles down on executions amid US-Israeli war
events.description: Iran has executed 14 individuals in recent weeks, with the hangings continuing unabated regardless of the US-Israeli military campaign against the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran doubles down on executions amid US-Israeli war

events.title: On Truth Social, die-hard MAGA supporters start to turn on Trump
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, April 10: We bring you world reactions after the US and Iran signed a fragile two-week ceasefire. Many papers condemn Donald Trump's claims of victory and highlight how once-fervent MAGA supporters are now openly criticising him on Truth Social. Also: a French magazine officialises far-right leader Jordan Bardella's rumoured relationship with an Italian socialite.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 On Truth Social, die-hard MAGA supporters start to turn on Trump

events.title: Wave of alleged migrant murders ignites fury across US as officials warn of more carnage, crackdown needed
events.description: A string of killings across the U.S. involving suspects in the country illegally is driving renewed outrage and calls for sweeping policy changes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wave of alleged migrant murders ignites fury across US as officials warn of more carnage, crackdown needed

events.title: Florida man arrested after allegedly burning his own home, killing man who tried to stop second blaze

events.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran

events.title: Morning news brief

events.title: Argentine MPs approve Milei-backed bill to allow mining in glaciers
events.description: Argentina's lower house of parliament on Thursday approved a bill backed by President Javier Milei to permit mining in glacier regions. Environmentalists and ​scientists have ‌warned that mining in in frozen parts of the Andes mountains would threaten water resources. The law takes effect once it is published in ‌the official gazette.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Argentine MPs approve Milei-backed bill to allow mining in glaciers

events.title: CAF’s Motsepe welcomes corruption probe after Senegal dispute
events.description: Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe on Wednesday said he would welcome a corruption investigation, adding the body had nothing to hide after meeting Senegalese officials in Dakar. Senegal last month called for a probe after CAF stripped it of the 2025 AFCON title and awarded it to Morocco.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 CAF’s Motsepe welcomes corruption probe after Senegal dispute

events.title: US-Iran truce: ‘Sensitive behind-the-scenes conversations’ paved way for Pakistan-brokered ceasefire
events.description: François Picard welcomes Mumtaz Zahra Baloch, Pakistan’s ambassador to France and Permanent Delegate to UNESCO. According to Baloch, the truce between Iran and the US is less about outright victory and more about survival – and a recalibration of power. She notes that what we are witnessing is not the end of a war, but the emergence of competing narratives that each side must sustain for political legitimacy: both sides claim success, yet neither has truly secured any semblance of victory.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran truce: ‘Sensitive behind-the-scenes conversations’ paved way for Pakistan-brokered ceasefire

events.title: Oil prices fall sharply after Trump announces two-week Iran war ceasefire
events.description: Oil prices have fallen sharply and stock markets have soared on news of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran.

events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea fires missiles toward sea after ridiculing South's hopes for better ties

events.title: Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes
events.description: The United Nations has strongly condemned airstrikes by the Israeli military across Lebanon on Wednesday which have resulted in significant casualties and destruction.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hundreds feared dead in Lebanon strikes

events.title: Husband taken into custody in connection with missing American woman last seen in Bahamas
events.description: The Coast Guard has opened a criminal investigation and Bahamas police have arrested Brian Hooker in connection to the disappearance of his wife, Lynette Hooker, during a Bahamas boat trip.

events.title: North Korea fires missiles toward sea after ridiculing South's hopes for better ties

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
events.description: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventually holding as many as 300 people in a fenced enclave with minimal contact with the outside world. He sexually abused and even tortured the children in the camp. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran war: Who gained ground, who lost influence
events.description: While civilians in the region pay the highest price, the Iran war has reshaped power balances far beyond the Gulf. Some states have gained leverage. Others have lost influence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Who gained ground, who lost influence

events.title: ‘We watched them die before our eyes’: Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages
events.description: As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan’s South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices – with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.

events.title: Baby among 3 dead in holiday horror as Easter egg hunt turns deadly
events.description: Three people were killed after high winds knocked down a tree onto a group of people in Germany during an Easter egg hunt on Sunday morning, police said.

events.title: Surging UK Green Party pushes church-state split, critics warn of break from Britain’s Christian roots
events.description: Critics slam the Green Party's vow to separate the Church of England from the state, calling it a direct assault on the nation's Christian heritage.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Surging UK Green Party pushes church-state split, critics warn of break from Britain’s Christian roots

events.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race

events.title: Trump meets NATO’s Rutte as US weighs exit over Iran war
events.description: US President Donald Trump held talks with NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday, the White House said, as he weighed leaving the alliance after it did not join the US in its war against Iran. The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump was also considering withdrawing US troops from some NATO countries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump meets NATO’s Rutte as US weighs exit over Iran war

events.title: Bahamian officials search for missing US woman after husband says she fell overboard
events.description: Authorities in the Bahamas are searching for a U.S. woman who is missing at sea after her husband reported that she fell overboard

events.title: Two Oklahoma elementary school workers charged with assaulting students
events.description: Two Elgin Elementary School employees face assault charges after allegedly attacking students, including a 5-year-old reportedly held in a chokehold.

events.title: UK's NATO show of force ends with docked destroyer in Mediterranean after 'technical' issue

events.description: Australia's most decorated living soldier Ben Roberts-Smith arrested and charged with allegedly murdering five unarmed Afghans in Afghanistan.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
events.description: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’ People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

events.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
events.description: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market. Continue reading...

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.description: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Iran threatens new hostilities as Israel pounds Lebanon
events.description: The two-week truce between the US, Israel and Iran has started showing cracks as the sides disagreed on Lebanon's inclusion in the ceasefire agreement. Follow DW for more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens new hostilities as Israel pounds Lebanon

events.title: Strategic Djibouti heads to polls in presidential vote
events.description: In tonight’s edition, Djibouti heads to the polls this weekend in a presidential election widely expected to extend President Ismaïl Omar Guelleh’s rule. Meanwhile, Pope Leo is set to become the first head of the Catholic Church to visit Algeria. And the Bijagos Archipelago in Guinea-Bissau has been added to UNESCO’s World Heritage List.

events.title: Scientists say the world's oldest octopus fossil isn't an octopus after all
events.description: Scientists have found evidence that a 300-million-year-old sea creature previously thought to be the world's oldest octopus is actually a nautilus relative

events.title: From misdiagnosis to medical bias: Why women are living longer but not better
events.description: For 25 years, the world has made significant progress in advancing women’s right to health, particularly in sexual and reproductive care. Women are living longer than ever before – but they are not living better.

events.title: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
events.description: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

events.title: Top Australian soldier charged with war crimes to remain in jail on remand
events.description: Ben Roberts-Smith faces five charges of the war crime of murder, which has a penalty of life in prison.

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Woman who allegedly attacked Sydney hospital patient with hammer claims he stole her brother’s ashes, court hears
events.description: Woman, 46, charged with grievous bodily harm after she allegedly struck 63-year-old RPA patient in head, NSW police say Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A man is fighting for his life after being allegedly attacked with a hammer at a Sydney hospital by a woman he knew who claimed he had stolen her brother’s ashes, a court has heard. Viki Graham, 46, was refused bail and will spend at least two months in jail after she was charged with wounding the 63-year-old man while he lay in a bed at Sydney’s Royal Prince Alfred hospital. Continue reading...

events.title: Once seen as a war hero, Australian ex-soldier will stand trial for alleged war crimes
events.description: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.

events.title: Matthew Perry's 'Ketamine Queen' dealer jailed for 15 years
events.description: Jasveen Sangha, a British-American dual citizen, had pleaded guilty to supplying the "Friends" star with the drugs that led to the actor's death in 2023.

events.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
events.description: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

events.title: PSG take control of Champions League semi-finals tie with win against Liverpool
events.description: PSG took a big step towards the Champions League semi-finals with a 2-0 win over Liverpool at home in the first leg. Désiré Doué opened the scoring in the 11th minute, before Khvicha Kvaratskhelia doubled the lead in the 65th minute. The reigning champions could even have regrets, given how much they dominated the match (74 percent possession, 18 shots).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 PSG take control of Champions League semi-finals tie with win against Liverpool

events.title: Pro-Iran groups have used AI to troll Trump and try to control the war narrative
events.description: Pro-Iran groups have used artificial intelligence to create slick internet memes in English to try to shape the narrative during the war against the U.S. and Israel, and foster opposition to it

events.title: Turkey detains 9 over attack outside the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul

events.title: ‘Entire families brutally erased’: Remembering the victims of the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda
events.description: Thirty-two years ago, a genocidal campaign was unleashed against Rwanda’s Tutsi minority, resulting in more than one million deaths. On Tuesday, the UN is holding commemorations to ensure that the genocide is never forgotten and never repeated.

events.title: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
events.description: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.

events.title: Michigan woman arrested for allegedly starving, torturing disabled sister-in-law she locked in basement
events.description: A Michigan woman was arrested after she allegedly kept her disabled sister-in-law locked in a basement for two years and nearly starved her to death.

events.title: Hawaii doctor learns fate after prosecutors say jealousy drove him to attack wife on cliffside hike
events.description: Gerhardt Konig, a doctor in Hawaii, was convicted of attempted manslaughter after trying to kill his wife on a hiking trail in Oahu in a bloody cliffside confrontation.

events.title: Athena Strand’s killer FedEx driver told police he ‘kind of tossed’ 7-year-old’s body into woods, video shows
events.description: Former FedEx driver Tanner Horner admitted to killing 7-year-old Athena Strand as jurors now decide between the death penalty or life in prison.

events.title: Charlotte light-rail murder suspect ruled incompetent to stand trial as history of crazed claims trails case
events.description: Decarlos Brown Jr., diagnosed with schizophrenia, was deemed incapable to proceed in the fatal stabbing of Iryna Zarutska on Charlotte's Lynx Blue Line.

events.title: Ex-paramedic accused of drip-feeding wife to death—then faking his own kidnapping when heat turned up
events.description: Joshua Hunsucker allegedly poisoned his wife with eye drops for a $250,000 life insurance policy. He now faces murder charges and staged kidnapping claim.

events.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
events.description: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at a rate more than double the city average. Break-ins and thefts remain the third highest per capita in Toronto. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood

events.title: British teen dies in Vietnam
events.description: A 19-year-old from the UK has died after a reported motorbike crash in Vietnam.
events.ticker_label: 💥 British teen dies in Vietnam

events.title: UN80 Initiative enters ‘delivery phase’, as Member States review progress on key work areas
events.description: Top officials updated Member States Monday on selected proposals under the UN80 reform initiative, including an initial assessment of a possible merger between gender equality agency, UN Women, and the UN reproductive health agency, UNFPA, as well as updates on the technology and data tracks.

events.title: School reinstates Pledge of Allegiance following lawsuit claiming students weren't given opportunity
events.description: A father-son duo is suing a Maine school district, claiming students were not given the opportunity to recite the Pledge of Allegiance for two years.

events.title: China aiding Iran missile program amid US-Israeli strikes, reports say
events.description: China is reportedly sending missile fuel materials to Iran to help rebuild its missile program amid continuing U.S.-Israeli strikes aimed against the Tehran regime.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
events.description: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March. Continue reading...

events.title: Djibouti election expected to extend incumbent’s 27 years in presidential office
events.description: Djibouti’s longstanding leader Ismael Omar Guelleh is expected to win Friday’s presidential election, handing him a sixth consecutive term in a race against one weak opposition candidate. While critics dismiss the election as “token competition”, supporters say the 78-year-old incumbent has brought stability to the Horn of Africa country strategically situated between the Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden.

events.title: Hong Kong firm files arbitration against Maersk, saying it schemed with Panama
events.description: A subsidiary of a Hong Kong-based conglomerate started arbitration proceedings against Danish logistics and port group Maersk, accusing the company of aligning with Panama and scheming to replace its port operation on the Central American country’s cri...

events.title: Rwanda tries to protect farmland in Africa's most densely populated nation
events.description: Rwanda has started to use satellite imagery and strict land rules to protect farmland in its capital, Kigali, as development expands

events.title: Former Fort Bragg employee charged with leaking classified military information to journalist

events.title: Iran releases 2 French citizens after 'three and a half years of detention,' Macron says

events.title: 'Credible intelligence' reveals North Korea's successor to Kim Jong Un, South Korea says
events.description: South Korea's National Intelligence Service thinks North Korean figure Kim Jong Un's daughter has been lined up to become Kim's eventual successor, lawmakers noted, according to Reuters.

events.title: Fury wants boxing clash with Joshua after comeback fight against Makhmudov
events.description: Fury, who returns to the ring against Makhmudov on Saturday, says he wants to face his compatriot Joshua next.

events.title: Russia, China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz, hours before Trump deadline
events.description: Russia and China vetoed a U.N. resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz hours before President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to stop threatening the vital waterway.

events.title: CENTCOM commander directed strike against an IRGC headquarters in underground facility: sources
events.description: U.S. Central Command Commander Admiral Brad Cooper directed a strike on an IRGC headquarters in an underground facility, sources indicated.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: India news: Kerala, Assam and Puducherry head to polls
events.description: It's election season in India with voters in two states and a union territory headed to polls. Meanwhile, Dhaka has once again asked India to extradite former Bangladeshi Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina. Follow DW

events.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
events.description: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: South Carolina pastor, wife arrested after alleged sexual, physical abuse of foster children
events.description: A South Carolina pastor and his wife were arrested after a foster child reported that the pastor sexually abused them while at the couple's home.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
events.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97

events.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
events.description: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from the past.

events.title: Japanese town sours on the crowds coming to see cherry blossoms and Mount Fuji

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses last-ditch legal effort to appeal defamation case against Network Ten and Lisa Wilkinson

The high court dismissed his attempt to challenge the outcome in a short judgment published to its website on Thursday. Continue reading...

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95

events.title: Hungarian election: How football has helped Orban keep power

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.description: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
events.description: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun makes rare China visit
